project_scope: Source-verified hospital readmission and quality intelligence dashboard

source_audit.note: Readmission measure mix shown for The Johns Hopkins Hospital; page shows data last updated May 13, 2026.
Dataset page shows Released May 13, 2026 and Last Modified January 26, 2026.
The Johns Hopkins Hospital has grade B for Spring 2026.
Licensed acute care beds including pediatric beds.

source_audit.source: Johns Hopkins News: Interventions Reduce Unnecessary Readmissions
source_audit.note: Reported 12.66% reduction in readmissions and $1.4M in avoided hospitalizations in 2016.

source_audit.as_of: Source page used for program descriptions
source_audit.note: Describes transition guides and Patient Access Line programs.

verified_facts.label: Readmission measure distribution
verified_facts.detail: Across readmission measures shown on Medicare Care Compare for The Johns Hopkins Hospital.

verified_facts.label: Latest federal public quality update used in this app
verified_facts.detail: Based on Medicare Care Compare visible update date and CMS HRRP release date.

verified_facts.label: Hospital beds
verified_facts.detail: Licensed acute care beds (including 204 pediatric beds) from Johns Hopkins Fast Facts.

official_interventions.name: Transition Guides
official_interventions.summary: Transition guides meet patients in the hospital and visit them at home for up to 30 days after discharge【40†L164-L168】.

official_interventions.name: Patient Access Line (PAL)
official_interventions.summary: Eligible patients receive a follow-up phone call from a nurse within two days after discharge【40†L176-L179】.

official_interventions.name: After-Care Clinic
official_interventions.summary: Clinic for high-risk patients that reduced readmissions and saved about $1.4M in 2016【39†L99-L100】.

disclaimer: This dashboard summarizes publicly available institutional quality and safety information. It does not provide diagnosis, treatment advice, or patient-level prediction.
